questions.q: What are the characteristics that distinguish a small business from a large business? (a) Limited resources, (b) Simple organisational structure, (c) Flexibility and adaptability, (d) All of the above
questions.answer: Small businesses are characterised by limited resources, simple organisational structure, and flexibility and adaptability. They have limited financial and human resources, which restricts their growth and expansion. Their organisational structure is simple, with fewer levels of management, which enables quick decision-making and response to changing market conditions. They are also highly flexible and adaptable, able to respond quickly to changes in the market or consumer demand. Therefore, option (d) All of the above is the correct answer.
marking_scheme: Mention 2-3 characteristics of small businesses (1 mark)
Explain how these characteristics affect the growth and expansion of small businesses (2 marks)
Provide examples of how small businesses can respond to changing market conditions (1 mark)
Justify why option (d) is the correct answer (1 mark)

questions.q: Explain the role of entrepreneurship in the development of small businesses. (a) Encouraging innovation and risk-taking, (b) Providing financial support, (c) Creating employment opportunities, (d) All of the above
questions.answer: Entrepreneurship plays a crucial role in the development of small businesses. Entrepreneurs are individuals who identify opportunities and create new businesses to capitalize on them. They encourage innovation and risk-taking, which leads to the development of new products, services, and processes. Entrepreneurs also provide financial support to small businesses by investing their own capital or seeking funding from external sources. Furthermore, entrepreneurship creates employment opportunities, as small businesses hire employees to help them grow and expand. Therefore, option (d) All of the above is the correct answer.
marking_scheme: Describe the role of entrepreneurship in identifying opportunities (1 mark)
Explain how entrepreneurs encourage innovation and risk-taking (1 mark)
Discuss the financial support provided by entrepreneurs to small businesses (1 mark)
Discuss the employment opportunities created by entrepreneurship (1 mark)
Justify why option (d) is the correct answer (1 mark)
questions.topic: Role of Entrepreneurship in Small Business

questions.q: What are the advantages of small businesses? (a) High profit margins, (b) Low start-up costs, (c) Flexibility and adaptability, (d) All of the above
questions.answer: Small businesses have several advantages. One of the main advantages is high profit margins, as they have lower production costs and can charge higher prices for their products. Another advantage is low start-up costs, as they require less capital to start and operate. Small businesses are also highly flexible and adaptable, able to respond quickly to changes in the market or consumer demand. This flexibility enables them to innovate and experiment with new products and services, which can lead to new business opportunities. Therefore, option (d) All of the above is the correct answer.
marking_scheme: Describe the high profit margins of small businesses (1 mark)
Explain the low start-up costs of small businesses (1 mark)
Discuss the flexibility and adaptability of small businesses (1 mark)
Justify why option (d) is the correct answer (1 mark)
Provide examples of how small businesses can innovate and experiment with new products and services (1 mark)

questions.q: What are the different types of small businesses? (a) Sole proprietorship, (b) Partnership, (c) Limited liability company, (d) All of the above
questions.answer: There are several types of small businesses, including sole proprietorship, partnership, and limited liability company. A sole proprietorship is a business owned and operated by one individual, with unlimited personal liability. A partnership is a business owned and operated by two or more individuals, with shared profits and losses. A limited liability company (LLC) is a business that provides limited personal liability to its owners, while also offering flexibility in ownership and management. Therefore, option (d) All of the above is the correct answer.
marking_scheme: Describe the characteristics of a sole proprietorship (1 mark)
Explain the characteristics of a partnership (1 mark)
Discuss the characteristics of a limited liability company (LLC) (1 mark)
Justify why option (d) is the correct answer (1 mark)
Provide examples of how each type of small business can be organized and operated (1 mark)

questions.q: Explain the importance of small businesses in a country's economy. (a) Providing employment opportunities, (b) Encouraging innovation and entrepreneurship, (c) Promoting economic growth and development, (d) All of the above
questions.answer: Small businesses play a vital role in a country's economy. They provide employment opportunities, as they hire employees to help them grow and expand. Small businesses also encourage innovation and entrepreneurship, as they are often the driving force behind new product development and process improvements. Furthermore, small businesses promote economic growth and development, as they contribute to the country's GDP and create new business opportunities. Therefore, option (d) All of the above is the correct answer.
marking_scheme: Discuss the role of small businesses in providing employment opportunities (1 mark)
Explain how small businesses encourage innovation and entrepreneurship (1 mark)
Discuss the contribution of small businesses to economic growth and development (1 mark)
Justify why option (d) is the correct answer (1 mark)
Provide examples of how small businesses can contribute to a country's economic development (1 mark)
questions.topic: Importance of Small Business in Economy
